name: Gambrel cut end wall
description: End-gable wall cut to the iconic two-stage gambrel profile: a steep lower pitch rising from the eaves that breaks at roughly sixty percent of total height into a shallower upper pitch climbing to the ridge. Unlike a single-slope gable triangle, the gambrel silhouette gives a generous, almost barn-like volume under the roof while keeping the visible facade lower and broader at the base. The wall is applied to the short faces of a rectangular footprint, mirrored on the opposite end, so the long faces remain straight and the building reads as a classic Dutch barn, Cape Cod cottage, or American colonial farmhouse from any angle. Built from horizontal courses of the main wall material with the stepped profile cut by removing voxels above the gambrel line, optionally trimmed with stairs along the two pitch edges.
